description: propose_followup throws `module './project-paths.js' does not provide an export named 'resolveRoadmapPath'` at runtime and writes nothing. Root cause: the long-running stdio compose MCP server caches a stale project-paths.js module record (loaded before COMP-PATHS-EXTERNAL added the export); propose_followup lazily `import()`s lib/followup-writer.js only on first call, which links against the stale cached module. On-disk code resolves cleanly (verified). Live fix: reconnect /mcp (reload server). Hardening: wrap the lazy import so a module-skew/missing-export error rethrows with an actionable "stale MCP server — reconnect /mcp" hint instead of a cryptic ESM error, and add a contract test asserting the followup-writer import graph resolves. Surfaced filing COMP-PARITY-3 follow-ups (fell back to add_roadmap_entry).

completions.notes: Actionable stale-server error around propose_followup's lazy import + import-graph contract test. Code deliverable complete; the live error clears on /mcp reconnect (stale running server). Contract test 2/2.
